item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Noting that I've restored the first image as it comes from here and, as indicated in this statement, is still CC0, thus being cc-compliant.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Currently, you have two unsourced images within your article. Please source these images by replying to the sent PM and/or this post within 48 hours or they will have to be deleted. Also, make sure the images comply with the SCP Wiki's CC-BY-SA 3.0 license by following this handy guide, otherwise they will have to be deleted.

Timer has expired, the images have been removed. Please PM with the source in order to have them restored.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I understand it the father of the subject becomes unable to perceive or remember the subject. No harm is done to the father of the subject.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's sad.
I get it, scp, secure it all, but im feeling for the kid.
I'd almost want this scp to go lost in some raid event and end up with horner.
Character building, nicely done.
Don't quite get what the hand does, but that's probably just me.
Anywho, solid +1.

EDIT:
So if someone holds the hand, their biological father temporarily vanishes for a while?
Now it's got me thinking, what if you do this but you were adopted…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a well done SCP. You told it through a clever and unique way of world-building. The chat-logs seem very human and realistic, helping the story's tone greatly. I couldn't get this anomaly out of my head once read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a nice little story. I like the realism of the chatlogs, and the sort of kindness it takes to do this while working around pacifism. Also, the dad's absurd traumatising piece works as a good satire of how abusive parents' actions seem totally bizarre to someone outside that situation (like Jude).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an interesting bit of worldbuilding, a touching/sad story, and a unique anomaly. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its good, but you could add another chat part were it shows the consequences of the Foundation intercepting the box.
